_meta.note: All figures harvested live from Bloomreach Engagement via the loomi MCP. Nothing is invented; empty findings are recorded as 0. The wobbly-donkey project is the standard Bloomreach fashion/jewellery demo dataset (real events, ~123k customers), NOT the synthetic PPD persona data in /data/personas.json. Counts via execute_analytics_eql; persona event histories via list_customer_events. 2026-05-27: persona event histories re-harvested via list_customer_events — high_value_returning swapped from dick.maser to jaylon.shinoda; marvin's purchase count corrected from 0 to 2 (he is an active buyer, incl. a $32,700 Cartier bracelet). 2026-05-28: cast recast to match real spend — Marvin moved gifting -> high_value_returning (the $32,700 purchase makes him high value), Jaylon dropped, and a new gifting persona (Jakob Minkus) was selected from live data (jewellery view_item in last 90 days + zero purchases). Elva unchanged. The diagnosis.* figures below are unchanged from the 2026-05-26 harvest.

autosegment_coverage.finding: 0 segmentations exist in the project. AutoSegment coverage = 0%.

ab_test_coverage.finding: 0 web-personalization experiments / A/B tests are configured. A/B coverage = 0%.
ab_test_coverage.note: 15988 raw 'experiment' events exist in event history, but there are 0 experiment DEFINITIONS in project config.

segment_definition_quality.finding: 2 segmentations exist, each with a single funnel step and a single price-threshold condition (purchase.total_price > $100 / $150). Neither is exposed to Discovery merchandising. Formula: avg(conditions_per_segment / 3) × (0.5 + 0.5 × discovery_exposed_ratio) = (1/3) × 0.5 = 0.167.

profile_completeness.method: execute_analytics_eql: 'select count customers matching customer.email has value by customer.country grouping top 5 with null'. Sum across all country rows (incl. null) = 25,187 customers with an email property populated, out of 123,164 total.
profile_completeness.finding: 10 customer properties are defined in the schema (city, country, email, external_score, first_name, last_name, phone, gender, language, is_internal_traffic) but only 25,187 of 123,164 customers (20.45%) have an email populated — the rest are anonymous cookies with no enriched profile.

behavioral_signal_richness.method: Schema breadth × per-user density proxy. 28 event types are defined (page_visit, view_category, view_item, cart_update, checkout, purchase, banner, campaign, session_start, session_end, registration, loyalty_update, support_ticket, return, store_visit, survey, experiment, retargeting, menu, paper_catalogue, first_session, installation, inbound_call, outbound_call, consent, user_active, merge, purchase_item). With 1,099,220 events spread across 13,010 90d-active customers, the average active user triggers ~4 distinct event types (SDK-auto session_start / session_end / page_visit plus 1 retail event). Formula: avg_distinct_types / target_distinct_types = 4 / 10 = 0.40.
behavioral_signal_richness.finding: 28 event types are defined and observed at least once, but the average 90d-active user only triggers ~4 distinct types — the rich behavioural fan-out (search, wishlist, survey, support_ticket, etc.) needed to drive multi-signal personalisation is not yet captured per user.

signal_freshness.most_recent_event_note: Events present 0 days ago (within 24h of harvest, e.g. 1447 events on the most recent day). Data is current, not stale.
signal_freshness.freshness_basis: Headline freshness = share of total customer base with >=1 event in the last 90 days (13010 / 123164 = 10.56%). Event recency itself is current (most recent event 0 days ago). 30-day active rate = 4.12%.
signal_freshness.method: execute_analytics_eql: 'select count customers by customer.country grouping top 50 with null customers matching exists[any event in last N days]', summed across all country rows (incl. null) to get distinct active customers.

recommendation_engines.finding: 0 recommendation engines exist, and nothing references the bounteous_fashion_jewellery_product catalog (50 items). The catalog is loaded but unused.

key_events: session_start: arrived via a Facebook 'sale' ad on a new mobile install (Welcome flow), Denver, ~2026-04-30
view_item: Cartier 'Cactus de Cartier Necklace' (Jewelery > women > necklaces, $4,200, 2026-04-30) — the gift signal, NOT purchased
view_item: Puma summer tights ($19.99), Qurvii top ($24.95), Mr Bowerbird men's jeans ($23.98), DressBerry trousers ($17.99)
cart_update: added DressBerry trousers — then abandoned
demo_personas.rationale: Real customer who arrived via a Facebook 'sale' ad on a new mobile install and, in a single recent session (~30 Apr 2026), browsed a $4,200 Cartier necklace alongside apparel and added trousers to his cart before leaving without buying anything. A live jewellery-browsing + cart-abandon signal with ZERO purchases -> a genuine Gifting Intent prospect. BRUID present, so Discovery can segment him.
demo_personas.caveat: Chosen from live MCP data (jewellery view_item in last 90 days + zero purchases, ranked top by recent engagement: 5 view_items). Replaces Marvin, who moved to High Value Returning once his real $32,700 Cartier purchase surfaced. Note: among zero-purchase customers this dataset has no heavier jewellery browser — one recent jewellery view + an abandoned cart is the strongest available real Gifting Intent example.

key_events: purchase: Cartier 'Love bracelet, diamond-paved' (Jewelery > bracelets, $32,700, card, 2025-08-13)
purchase: DressBerry trousers (Apparel > women > pants, $17.99, PayPal, 2025-06-10)
loyalty_update: points earned → final 210 GOLD (later 170 silver)
view_item: Cartier 'C de Cartier wedding band' (Jewelery > rings, $2,680, 2025-08-29) — browsed, not purchased
view_item: Jockey thermal top (Apparel > women > tops, $6.79)
banner: 'Free delivery' (click), 'Onexit banner with last viewed items' (show/close)
campaign: Black Friday, Post-purchase NPS flow; consent: accept newsletter
demo_personas.rationale: Identified, BRUID-present Denver shopper with 47 events and TWO completed purchases — a $17.99 DressBerry trouser and a $32,700 Cartier 'Love bracelet, diamond-paved' — plus loyalty membership and post-purchase NPS engagement. By spend he is the highest-value customer in the cast: a clear High Value Returning customer who also browses premium jewellery (a Cartier wedding band most recently).
demo_personas.caveat: Recast from Gifting Intent to High Value Returning to match real spend (the $32,700 Cartier purchase). Replaces Jaylon (customer_id 6a0ded44355ecedd103debda, $186.98 LUXURAZI suit + Gold loyalty), who was dropped from the demo cast.

key_events: banner: 'Push notifications subscription banner' (Variant A, show) — 2025-10-07
demo_personas.rationale: Only 2 lifetime events - a banner impression then a registration on the same day. Brand-new account with essentially zero behavioral signal. Would fall into a New Prospecting segment.

would_be_segments_definition.Gifting Intent: Customers browsing/viewing gift-category items (e.g. jewellery, wedding bands) without recent purchase intent of their own.
would_be_segments_definition.High Value Returning: Identified customers with completed purchase(s) and loyalty membership / repeat lifecycle engagement.
would_be_segments_definition.New Prospecting: Newly registered or near-zero-signal customers with minimal event history.
